answer:
The voltage across a time-varying inductor is given by \( v(t) = L(t) \frac{di}{dt} + i(t) \frac{dL}{dt} \).  
Here, \( L(t) = L_0 (t + \tanh t) \), so \( \frac{dL}{dt} = L_0 (1 + \operatorname{sech}^2 t) \), and \( i(t) = \cos \omega t \), so \( \frac{di}{dt} = -\omega \sin \omega t \).  
Substituting gives \( v(t) = L_0 (1 + \operatorname{sech}^2 t) \cos \omega t - \omega L_0 (t + \tanh t) \sin \omega t \), which matches option C.

\boxed{C}
